title: Fixing Stable Bubble Sort with Incorrect Comparison

statement.markdown: You are given a list of integers. Sort this list using a stable bubble sort algorithm. Write the implementation of the `solve` method in the `Solution` class.
statement.inputFormat: The input format will be a single line with a list of integers separated by spaces.
statement.outputFormat: The output format will be the sorted list of integers separated by spaces.
statement.notes: Use a stable sorting algorithm to sort the list. The sorting order should be non-decreasing.

examples.explanation: The numbers in the input are sorted in non-decreasing order.

hints.text: Use a stable sorting algorithm like bubble sort to sort the list.
Check for the correct number of swaps in each pass.

editorial.title: Fixing Stable Bubble Sort with Incorrect Comparison
editorial.intuition: The stable bubble sort algorithm has a logical bug in its comparison.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach uses a simple nested loop to check each element and perform a bubble sort.
editorial.betterSolution: The better solution is to use a stable sorting algorithm.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a stable sorting algorithm with a correct comparison.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the mathematical analysis of the bubble sort algorithm.
editorial.dryRun: The dry run is based on the input "1 3 2 4 5"
